last_completed_round.direction_name: Human idea async copy: split hot-band copy ownership so lower warps stage A and upper warps stage B
last_completed_round.hypothesis: The hot-band kernel still dominates and the current branch remains occupancy-limited but feed-sensitive. The user explicitly asked to keep pushing coalescing / async-copy ideas without falling back into the dedicated producer-only branch that already failed. A concrete middle ground is to keep all eight warps in the CTA and the same two-stage pipeline, but split copy ownership inside the staging helpers so the lower four warps own contiguous A stripes and the upper four warps own contiguous B stripes. That reduces simultaneous A/B copy traffic from every warp while avoiding permanent producer-only roles.

notes: Build passed for round 10/30. Node A will measure the result next.
